meta.title: Heading
meta.description: The heading component establishes typographic hierarchy throughout the product experience. It is crucial for organizing content, defining page structure, and improving content scan ability.

mdx:
## Overview

Good headings are essential for organizing information and engaging users. They
clearly communicate the content's topic, making the page scannable and easier to
navigate. Effective headlines also improve accessibility for screen readers and
aid search engine optimization (SEO) by defining content relevance.

### Resources

Deep dive on details and access design library.

[Figma library](https://www.figma.com/design/gHbAJGfcrCv7f2bgzUQgHq/NIMBUS-Guidelines?node-id=5373-117&focus-id=5373-92&m=dev)

## Variables

Get familiar with the features.

#### Typographic scale and hierarchy

These text styles are the recommended styles for headings.

| **Category**   | **Description**                                               |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Heading H1** | Primary page titles and largest headers.                      |
| **Heading H2** | Used for secondary headlines.                                 |
| **Heading H3** | Used for tertiary headlines.                                  |
| **Heading H4** | Commonly used for section headers and large accordion titles. |

a11y.mdx:
## Accessibility

Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by
everyone, including people with disabilities, by addressing visual, auditory,
cognitive, and physical limitations.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="400">
    <Heading as="h1" size="4xl">Heading H1</Heading>
    <Heading as="h2" size="3xl">Heading H2</Heading>
    <Heading as="h3" size="2xl">Heading H3</Heading>
    <Heading as="h4" size="xl">Heading H4</Heading>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Accessibility standards

- **Semantic markup:** Use the correct HTML heading tags (`<h1>` to `<h6>`) to
  establish a document outline, rather than relying on font styling alone.
- **Logical order:** Maintain the correct hierarchical order (e.g., `<h2>`
  follows `<h1>`, not `<h4>`).
- **Informative text:** Ensure the headline clearly describes the content that
  immediately follows it for easy scanning.
- **Contrast:** Maintain a high contrast ratio (`3:1` for large text, `4.5:1`
  for smaller/regular text) between the text and background.
- **Visual distinction:** Ensure the headline is visually distinct (size,
  weight) from body text to aid quick visual scanning.
- **Language attribute:** Use the lang attribute if the headline is in a
  language different from the rest of the page.

guidelines.mdx:
## Guidelines

Using headings is a good way to keep content accessible and formatted for those
who use screen readers but also helps visual users understand the format of the
page and easily scan to find the needed sections efficiently.

### Best practices

- **Keep hierarchy consistent**: Use a limited, predefined set of text styles to
  maintain visual consistency across all products. Add custom styles with
  caution.
- **Prioritize line length**: Use optimal line lengths (typically 50 to 75
  characters per line) to prevent eye strain and facilitate reading.
- **Use concise language**: Write clearly, avoiding unnecessary jargon or overly
  long sentences to reduce cognitive load.
- **Break up content**: Use short paragraphs, lists, and headings to break up
  large blocks of text, making content easier to scan.
- **Maintain consistent tone**: The language, style, and formality should be
  consistent throughout the application, reflecting the brand's voice.
- **Handle truncation gracefully**: When necessary, truncate long text with
  ellipses and provide a tooltip or interaction element to reveal the full
  content.
- **Keep length in mind for translation**: When supporting text localization,
  length can expand more than 20%. Design experiences that can be flexible to
  shortening and lengthening of content.

### Header Do's and Don'ts

> [!TIP]\
> **Do**
>
> - Use predefined headings, body sizes, and weights to clearly signal the
>   importance of content.
> - Always maintain sufficient contrast between text and its background to meet
>   WCAG AA standards (4.5:1 minimum).
> - Headings should be short and aid users when scanning sections.
> - Ensure adequate vertical spacing (line-height) to prevent text lines from
>   colliding and improve readability.
> - Write clearly, avoiding unnecessary jargon and overly long sentences.

> [!CAUTION]\
> **Don't**
>
> - Avoid introducing too many different font sizes, weights, or families, which
>   destroys visual consistency.
> - Never use color as the only method to convey meaning (e.g., indicating an
>   error solely with red text).
> - Avoid excessively long lines of text (over 80 characters), which makes
>   tracking difficult and causes fatigue.
> - Avoid line heights that are too small, which makes text visually dense and
>   difficult to read.
> - Never truncate text without providing a clear way (like a tooltip) to view
>   the full content.
> - Avoid pairing text colors that barely distinguish themselves from the
>   background, even if they look "stylish."
